LAW AND MOTION 302, LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS PATRICIA SCHAEFER AND JEFFREY CRANDALL'S MOTION SEEKING LEAVE TO AMEND. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 6, 2008. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE A PLEADING (NOT TO EXCEED 10 PAGES) BASED ON WHAT HAS BEEN SUBMITTED IN CONNECTION WITH THE MOTION. PLEADING TO BE FILED BY JULY 25, 2008 AND ADDRESS THE FOLLOWING ISSUES: 1- REFER TO THE SPECIFIC FACTS PLAINTIFFS LEARNED, WHEN THEY FIRST LEARNED THEM, IF THE FACTS LEARNED EMERGED FROM THE PARTIES ONGOING DISCOVERY EFFORTS; 2- WHAT PARTICULAR FACTS RELATE TO NEW CAUSES OF ACTION THAT PLAINTIFFS WANT TO ALLEGE; 3- ELEMENTS OF THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION AS WELL AS THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS THAT APPLIES. DEFENDANT TO FILE A REPLY BY AUGUST 4, 2008. ORDER REGARDING EX PARTE APPLICATION ON SUMMARY JUDGMENT PAGE LENGTH SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Ntc Of Pltfs Motion Seeking Leave To Amend
Off Calendar Aug-06-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS PATRICIA SCHAEFER AND JEFFREY CRANDALL'S MOTION SEEKING LEAVE TO AMEND

Appearances:
Eric Gale of Bradley Curley Asiano Barrabee & Gale (464.8888 x244) for defendants and responding parties
Yosef Peretz of Kletter & Peretz (732.3777) for plaintiffs and moving parties

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: denied with respect to causes of action 12 to 16. Plaintiff's have not met the requisite showing of either when the facts were discovered that support such claims and the facts plaintiffs point to fail to support the causes of action alleged. Granted with respect to the 17th cause of action for abuse of process and the 18th cause of action for slander. Facts are alleged. Any challenge to the causes of action under CCP 425.16 must be raised by motion. Ten days leave to amend. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: the matter is continued to August 6, 2008.
